paper_title_zh: A Rapid Multiplex Assay for Pathogen Detection
paper_title_en: A Rapid Multiplex Assay for Pathogen Detection
research_direction: 数字微流控与多重核酸检测
one_sentence_summary: This paper presents a multiplex rapid assay that combines target amplification, spatial encoding, and fluorescence readout for low-resource pathogen detection.
research_problem: Single-target assays are inefficient for co-infection scenarios and require repeated testing.
method_route: and used threshold rules to call positive reactions.
key_findings: The assay distinguished multiple targets in a single workflow.
The encoded readout reduced interpretation complexity and worked on simulated samples.
Overall workflow from sample input to encoded fluorescence readout.
